title: phyllanthus indicus
intro: Phyllanthus indicus, also known as Airy Shaw's Phyllanthus or Amla, is a plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Phyllanthus indicus include its ability to improve liver function, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of diabetes, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. Phyllanthus indicus is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to prepare herbal supplements and tonics.
